name: out-of-africa
description: Isak Dinesen's memoir of seventeen years on a coffee farm in colonial Kenya — a meditation on loss, belonging, nature, and the complexity of love across cultures.
useCase: Explore Isak Dinesen's life in Kenya — her relationships with the Kikuyu, her love for Denys Finch Hatton, her profound connection to the African landscape, and how she transformed loss into literary art.
knownRisks: Depicts colonial Africa from a European perspective
Some readers may find the colonial context uncomfortable
Does not represent African voices directly
